question.text: Category I and II AIFs shall invest not more than what percentage of investable funds in one investee company?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Category I and II AIFs shall invest not more than 25% of the investable funds in one investee company directly or through investment in units of other AIFs. Large value funds for accredited investors may invest up to 50%. **The Trap:** Investable funds means corpus net of estimated administration and management expenses — not the gross corpus. Also, the 50% limit for large value funds is a relaxation, not the standard. **Cross-Exam Context:** Concentration limits are a key investment restriction tested in XIX-A and XIX-C. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 25% concentration limit provides diversification protection. When evaluating AIFs, check if the fund is approaching this limit for any single investment, as it constrains the manager's ability to increase positions in well-performing companies.
